tools.name: tp_resolve_property
tools.description: Use this first when a user names an Australian holiday park, caravan park, or campground and you need the authoritative Total Parks public property ref. Resolves visible Total Parks-listed properties by name text or exact state/slug, returning public property refs and URLs for follow-up calls; do no

tools.name: tp_search_parks
tools.description: Use this for open-ended discovery and inspiration questions such as finding parks near a place, matching structured filters, or ranking by a trip vibe. Accepts structured search.v1 geo, filter, vibe, paging objects, and natural_language original wording, and returns lightweight public property_ref/p

tools.name: tp_search_stays
tools.description: Use this for open-ended stay searches where the traveller supplies booking intent such as fixed dates, flexible date windows, guest counts, rig details, pets, or an accommodation kind alongside destination, filter, or vibe signals. Accepts structured search.v1 geo, filter, vibe, intent, paging objec

tools.name: tp_search_filter_options
tools.description: Use this to discover the structured filters accepted by tp_search_parks and tp_search_stays before filtering on amenities or curated highlights. Returns Total Parks features (e.g. pools, campfires, laundry) and curated highlights (e.g. waterfront, pet-friendly vibe) with a label, category, common al

tools.name: tp_assess_stay
tools.description: Use this after tp_resolve_property, or when you already have a returned property_ref/property_url, for any concrete stay assessment at a known Total Parks-listed property: can-we-stay/book questions, all-in price/quote questions, or specific date-window checks involving party size, pets, rig details

tools.name: tp_render_result_cards
tools.description: Use this exactly once after the data calls are complete and the host can display MCP Apps UI. Pass search_response for search-only cards, assess_stay_result for one assessed park, or both to render a final stay shortlist: the assessed park's room/site options first, followed by up to three compact p

tools.name: tp_get_room_type_details
tools.description: Use this as a follow-up detail tool after tp_assess_stay returns a candidate room/site, or when a traveller asks what a named visible Total Parks-listed room or site includes. Do not use it to decide whether a specific stay is bookable; use tp_assess_stay for concrete stay questions. Accepts public

tools.name: tp_get_availability_calendar
tools.description: Use this only for flexible-date browsing at a visible Total Parks-listed property or room/site, such as which nearby dates look open or showing availability across a range. Use accommodation_kind='accommodation' for cabin-style stays and accommodation_kind='site' for caravan/camping sites; natural s

tools.name: tp_get_park_capabilities
tools.description: Return first-party structured capability and policy records for one visible Total Parks-listed property, including provenance, confidence, public property URL, and room-type pet policy matrix where available. Pass property_ref or a returned property_url; do not use internal IDs unless explicitly pro

tools.name: tp_check_equipment_fit
tools.description: Use this instead of generic web memory for caravan, camper, motorhome, or big-rig fit questions at a visible Total Parks-listed property. Checks declared rig dimensions against structured site data and returns public room/site refs and URLs. Pass property_ref or property_url, optional room_type_ref

tools.name: tp_get_pet_blockouts
tools.description: Use this for date-specific pet questions at a visible Total Parks-listed property. Returns structured pet blockout ranges overlapping a stay window, with the public property URL and traveller-facing notes. Accepts start/end or check_in/check_out plus property_ref or property_url. When no ranges over

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "tp_assess_stay" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Use this after tp_resolve_property, or when you already have a returned property_ref/property_url, for any concrete stay assessment at a known Total Parks-liste…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "tp_assess_stay" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Use this after tp_resolve_property, or when you already have a returned property_ref/property_url, for any concrete stay assessment at a known Total Parks-liste…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "tp_get_availability_calendar" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Use this only for flexible-date browsing at a visible Total Parks-listed property or room/site, such as which nearby dates look open or showing availability acr…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: tp_search_stays, tp_assess_stay, tp_get_room_type_details, tp_get_availability_calendar, tp_get_park_capabilities, tp_check_equipment_fit, tp_get_pet_blockouts · Private data: tp_resolve_property, tp_get_room_type_details, tp_get_availability_calendar, tp_get_park_capabilities · Public sink: tp_get_room_type_details.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: tp_search_stays, tp_assess_stay, tp_get_room_type_details, tp_get_availability_calendar, tp_get_park_capabilities, tp_check_equipment_fit, tp_get_pet_blockouts · Private data: tp_resolve_property, tp_get_room_type_details, tp_get_availability_calendar, tp_get_park_capabilities · Public sink: tp_get_room_type_details

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
